quality.clarity: High clarity. Mechanics of upward and downward fall are well-defined, including the effect at the end of the duration and the saving throw.
quality.balance_assessment: High-impact control spell. The 50-foot radius is significant, and forcing a save just to avoid immediate upward movement is powerful, though concentration limits repeated use. The upward movement to the ceiling is a unique form of incapacitation/control.

mechanics.initial_effect: Upward movement for unsecured objects/creatures to the top of the cylinder.
mechanics.mitigation: Dexterity saving throw to grab an anchor point.
mechanics.collision_handling: Strikes the ceiling as if falling normally.
mechanics.end_of_duration_effect: Objects/creatures at the top fall back down.
mechanics.interaction_notes: Requires line of sight (implied by range). Components V, S, M are standard for a 7th-level spell. Concentration limits sustained control.

suggested_refinements.suggestion: Explicitly state if the Dexterity save is made against the spell save DC or if it is an ability check to grab an object. (Assuming standard spell save DC based on common TTRPG conventions, but explicit clarification is beneficial for archival).
